$schema_note: Pricing constants for Phase 4C dual-track buyer math (Bible v3 §6 + Briefing §14 #6). All values are working assumptions per the truth hierarchy (§17) — edit JSON to override, no code change required. Each value should be revisited as real deals close.
source: Briefing §14 #6 (proposed $10K wholesale floor) + Bible v3 buyer math + 5/12 working assumptions for unresolved parameters

wholesale_fee._note: Briefing §14 #6: '$10K floor confirmed across all markets, or market-tiered?' — encoding $10K as default pending Alex confirmation. Old arv-validate code used $15K as placeholder.

closing_costs._note: Buyer-side closing as a fraction of ARV. 13% covers commission + title + financing + holding costs over a 3-4 month flip cycle. Source: existing arv-validate placeholder.

flipper_track._note: Buyer-profit target the cash flipper needs to make the deal worth doing. $30K is the existing arv-validate placeholder; Bible v3 references 'reasonable flip profit' without naming a number. Refine against real flipper sales.

landlord_track._note: Landlord buyers accept thinner upfront margins because the return comes from cashflow + appreciation. $5K is a working assumption — landlords typically buy at NOI/cap and don't expect day-one rehab equity. Revisit once we close a landlord deal.

dual_track_decision._note: Sturtevant case: if YourMAO_Landlord exceeds YourMAO_Flipper by more than this dollar threshold, flag the deal as a creative-finance candidate (landlord buyer + seller-financed because flipper math is too thin). $5K threshold avoids flagging marginal differences.
